text: {"claims":[{"statement":"An egg breakfast leads to a significantly higher net protein balance and reduced protein breakdown compared with a cereal breakfast.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"egg breakfast led to a significantly higher net protein balance and reduced protein breakdown compared with a cereal breakfast","entailment":"The quoted result directly reports higher net protein balance and lower protein breakdown after an egg breakfast versus a cereal breakfast.","population":"adults (57–74 y)","intervention":"egg breakfast (protein matched)","comparator":"cereal breakfast (protein matched)","outcome":"net protein balance and protein breakdown","timeframe":"acute","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Hand grip strength and strength increased significantly in the egg group of an elderly women study.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"hand grip strength and strength increased significantly in the egg group","entailment":"The quote states that hand grip strength and overall strength rose significantly among participants receiving egg white protein.","population":"elderly women","intervention":"egg white protein supplement","comparator":"carbohydrate supplement","outcome":"hand grip strength and overall strength","timeframe":"6 months","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Both a high protein egg-containing diet and a low protein egg-free diet for 12 weeks reduced body weight and body fat.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"consumption of both a high protein egg-containing diet or a low protein egg-free diet for 12 weeks reduced body weight and body fat","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports weight and fat loss after 12 weeks on either diet.","population":"adults (50–80 y)","intervention":"high protein egg-containing diet; low protein egg-free diet","comparator":"","outcome":"body weight and body fat","timeframe":"12 weeks","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Only participants on the high protein egg diet maintained lean mass while lean mass declined on the low protein egg-free diet.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"only those on the high protein diet maintained lean mass while this declined on the low protein diet","entailment":"The quote indicates a differential effect on lean mass between the high protein egg diet and the low protein egg-free diet.","population":"adults (50–80 y)","intervention":"high protein diet (3 eggs/day)","comparator":"low protein egg-free diet","outcome":"lean mass maintenance","timeframe":"12 weeks","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Whole egg significantly increased post‑exercise myofibrillar protein synthetic response compared with egg white.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Whole egg significantly increased post-exercise myofibrillar protein synthetic response vs. egg white","entailment":"The quoted result shows a greater myofibrillar protein synthesis after whole egg intake versus egg white.","population":"men (21 ± 1 y)","intervention":"whole egg (protein matched)","comparator":"egg white (protein matched)","outcome":"post‑exercise myofibrillar protein synthesis","timeframe":"acute","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Whole egg diet significantly reduced percent body fat by 2.7% compared with egg white diet.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Whole egg diet significantly reduced percent body fat (−2.7%) and increased hand and quadriceps strength and serum testosterone vs. egg white diet","entailment":"The quote reports a 2.7% reduction in body fat after a whole egg diet versus an egg‑white diet.","population":"men (24.6 ± 2.7 y)","intervention":"whole egg diet","comparator":"egg white diet","outcome":"percent body fat","timeframe":"12 weeks","quantifiers":"−2.7%","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Whole‑body net protein balance was significantly greater after animal protein diets than after plant protein diets.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Whole-body net protein balance significantly greater following animal protein diets vs. plant protein diets","entailment":"The quoted statement directly compares net protein balance between animal and plant protein sources, showing a higher value for animal proteins.","population":"adults (18–40 y)","intervention":"animal protein diets (including eggs)","comparator":"plant protein diets","outcome":"whole‑body net protein balance","timeframe":"acute","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"High pork and eggs suppressed protein breakdown to a greater extent than high mixed nuts.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Compared with high mixed nuts, high pork and eggs suppressed protein breakdown to a greater extent","entailment":"The quote indicates that protein breakdown was more strongly suppressed after pork and egg consumption than after mixed nuts.","population":"adults (18–40 y)","intervention":"high pork and eggs","comparator":"high mixed nuts","outcome":"protein breakdown","timeframe":"acute","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Hand grip strength and number of arm curls significantly increased from baseline in the egg group only.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Hand grip strength and number of arm curls significantly increased from baseline in egg group only","entailment":"The quoted result shows improvements in hand grip strength and arm curls exclusively in participants receiving egg supplementation.","population":"females (73.6 ± 8.3 y)","intervention":"egg white protein supplement","comparator":"carbohydrate supplement","outcome":"hand grip strength and arm curls","timeframe":"6 months","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"No significant differences in body composition were observed between egg and control groups.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"No significant differences in body composition between groups","entailment":"The quote explicitly states that body composition did not differ significantly between the intervention and control groups.","population":"elderly women; females (73.6 ± 8.3 y)","intervention":"egg white protein supplement","comparator":"carbohydrate supplement","outcome":"body composition","timeframe":"6 months","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"}]}
